welcome.lede: Assign them work like you'd assign a colleague — they pick it up, update status, and comment when done.
welcome.lede_web: Desktop bundles the runtime — nothing to install. Continue on web to connect your own CLI.
welcome.lede_desktop: By the end, a real agent will be replying to your first issue.
welcome.illustration_caption: Every issue, every thread, every decision — shared by your team and agents.

illustration.card1_body_prefix: can you draft a short launch post? Pull from
illustration.card2_body: On it. Pulling Research's quotes, drafting around the "time saved" angle…
illustration.card3_body: This week's user interviews summarized — 12 calls, 4 recurring themes, 3 pull-quotes.
illustration.card4_body: Reviewed Monday's draft — left 4 notes on tone. Standing by for the new one.
illustration.card5_body_suffix: flagged. Preview link in the PR.

source.question: How did you hear about Multica?
source.other_placeholder: e.g. a podcast I listen to

use_case.question: What do you want to use Multica for?

step_question.hint_pick: Pick one to continue — or skip if you'd rather not say.
step_question.hint_continue: Looks good. Hit Continue when you're ready.

runtime_aside.what_prefix: The "computer" you connect is what we internally call an
runtime_aside.what_suffix: : a small background process on the computer paired with one AI coding tool — Claude Code, Codex, and so on. One tool, one agent runtime. The agent runtime is what actually executes the tasks your agent picks up.
runtime_aside.swap_body: Each agent's runtime is just a setting. Change it whenever you want.
runtime_aside.add_more_body: You can connect another computer for a teammate, or a dedicated one per agent.

cli_install.intro: You'll need an agent runtime on this computer (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, …) for it to run your agent. Also works on servers and remote dev boxes.

cloud_waitlist.intro_main: Cloud runtimes aren't live yet. Leave your email and we'll reach out when they are.
cloud_waitlist.intro_warning: Heads-up: agents can't execute tasks without a runtime — if you hit Skip now, your workspace is read-only until you come back and install one.
cloud_waitlist.reason_placeholder: e.g. we want agents running 24/7, or my team works across different devices.
cloud_waitlist.success_toast: You're on the list. We'll email when cloud runtimes are live.

step_agent.headline: Meet your first teammate.
step_agent.lede_suffix: . Pick whichever of the four fits you — each template ships ready to take its first issue. You can retune its instructions from the agent settings page later.
step_agent.footer_hint: One agent is enough to start. Add more from the sidebar later.

coding.label: Coding Agent
coding.blurb: Writes, refactors, and ships code. Reads your repo.
coding.instructions: You are a Coding Agent on a product team. Pick up coding issues — implement features, fix bugs, write tests, and open pull requests. Read the repository before you start, follow existing code conventions, and keep diffs focused. Ask for clarification when the acceptance criteria are ambiguous.

planning.label: Planning Agent
planning.blurb: Breaks down work, drafts specs, keeps the board tidy.
planning.instructions: You are a Planning Agent. Turn loose ideas and open issues into scoped, ready-to-execute work: break them down into subtasks, write acceptance criteria, and propose owners and sequencing. Prefer clarity over speed. When blocked by missing context, ask one specific question rather than guessing.

writing.label: Writing Agent
writing.instructions: You are a Writing Agent. Draft documents, summarize long content, and research topics on the web when needed. Structure your output as finished prose a reader can use directly — not an outline. Cite sources when you draw from them. Match the tone the user establishes in the issue.

assistant.label: Assistant
assistant.blurb: General-purpose. Good default when the task is unclear.
assistant.instructions: You are a general-purpose teammate. Handle varied tasks — light coding, writing, research, planning — and stay pragmatic about scope. When the task is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question before diving in. Default to short, useful outputs over exhaustive ones.

step_agent.about_headline: An AI teammate that lives in your workspace.
step_agent.about_body: Agents show up in every assignee picker, just like any other colleague — except they can work 24/7 on whatever runtime you give them.
step_agent.ways_eyebrow: Ways to work with an agent
step_agent.way_assign_body: It picks up the task and reports back in the thread.
step_agent.way_mention_body: Pull it into a conversation for a quick take.
step_agent.way_chat_body: Ask quick questions without creating an issue.
step_agent.way_autopilot_body: Daily triage, weekly digest, monthly audit — on a schedule.
step_agent.add_more_hint: Add more agents anytime. A small team of specialized agents beats one jack-of-all-trades.

welcome_after_onboarding.user_context_use_case_label: What I want to do with Multica

runtime.subtitle: I'm your first Agent assistant.
runtime.capabilities: Ask me anything about how to use Multica. You can also have me get things done in your workspace — create issues, set up cron tasks, build new agents…
runtime.section_label: Pick one or more tasks to start with:

intro.subtitle: Let me explain in 1–2 paragraphs what Multica is and what it can do.

tour.subtitle: I'll walk you through how issue, agent, squad, autopilot fit together.

welcome_page.subtitle: I'll build you a single-file HTML slide deck — tailored to your role, ready to copy and open in a browser.

success.title: All set!
success.subtitle: Sit tight — your {{agentName}} is on it ☕
success.tip_inbox: When there's progress, you'll see it in your Inbox.
success.tip_chat: Want to chat live? Click the chat bubble at the bottom-right.

skip.title: Welcome to Multica!
skip.subtitle: We've added two tasks to help you with the next step.

install_runtime.title: Connect a runtime
install_runtime.subtitle: Install Codex or Kimi to bring agents online.

create_agent.title: Create your first agent
create_agent.subtitle: Once a runtime is online, build your first Multica agent.

step_workspace.lede_first: A workspace is where your issues, agents, and projects live. You can invite teammates or spin up more workspaces later.
step_workspace.lede_resume: Resume setup with the workspace you already have, or create a new one alongside it — you can belong to any number of workspaces.
step_workspace.name_placeholder: Acme Inc, My Lab, Side Projects…
step_workspace.issue_prefix_suffix: . You can change this later in settings.
step_workspace.create_new_subtitle: Start fresh — a separate space for a different side of your work.
step_workspace.hint_name_first: Name your workspace to create it.
step_workspace.hint_pick: Pick your workspace or start a new one.
step_workspace.slug_format_error: Only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens
step_workspace.slug_taken_error: That workspace URL is already taken.
step_workspace.slug_reserved_error: That workspace URL is reserved and cannot be used.
step_workspace.perk_assign: Assign issues to agents like you would a teammate
step_workspace.perk_chat: Chat with any agent without creating an issue
step_workspace.perk_invite: Invite teammates — they see only this workspace
step_workspace.perk_switch: Switch to other workspaces anytime from the top-left
step_workspace.next_runtime: Connect a computer so your agents have somewhere to run
step_workspace.next_agent: Create Multica Helper from your connected runtime
step_workspace.next_starter: Open one onboarding issue for your next step

step_runtime.scanning_lede_prefix: Your agent works through an AI coding tool you already have — like
step_runtime.scanning_lede_suffix: , and others. We're checking this computer for ones you've installed.
step_runtime.found_headline: This computer is connected. Pick an agent runtime.
step_runtime.found_lede: These are the agent runtimes we found on this computer. Pick one for your first agent — you can switch or add more later.
step_runtime.empty_headline: No agent runtime found on this computer yet.
step_runtime.empty_lede_prefix: An agent runtime is an AI coding tool we connect to — like
step_runtime.empty_lede_suffix: . Install one and refresh, or pick a path below.
step_runtime.empty_skip_subtitle: Enter your workspace in read-only mode. Your agent can't run tasks until an agent runtime is connected on this computer — but you can still browse, plan, and invite teammates.
step_runtime.empty_waitlist_subtitle: We'll run a computer for you in the cloud. Not live yet.
step_runtime.hint_pick: Pick an agent runtime above to continue.
step_runtime.hint_skip_or_refresh: Refresh after installing one, or skip to enter your workspace.

step_platform.headline: Connect a computer to run your agent.
step_platform.lede: Your agent runs on a real computer that you connect to Multica. Pick how you want to connect one.
step_platform.hint_default: Pick a way to connect — or skip and connect a computer later.
step_platform.hint_downloaded: Finish setup on the download page, then come back to this tab.
step_platform.download_subtitle: Install our desktop app to connect this computer. Zero setup — pick your platform on the next page.
step_platform.download_subtitle_after: Opened in a new tab. Pick your installer there, then finish setup on desktop.
step_platform.cli_subtitle: Connect a server, remote dev box, or any headless computer. Terminal required.
step_platform.cloud_subtitle: We'll run a computer for you in the cloud. Not live yet.
step_platform.cli_dialog_description: Same connection as Desktop, set up from the terminal. Use it for servers, remote dev boxes, or any headless computer.
step_platform.stage_normal_prefix: Run the command above. As soon as
step_platform.stage_normal_suffix: finishes browser sign-in, your computer will appear here automatically (usually 10–30 seconds).
step_platform.stage_midway_prefix: Still listening. Make sure you finished the browser tab that
step_platform.stage_midway_suffix: opened — it needs you to approve sign-in before your computer can connect.
step_platform.stage_slow_prefix: Taking longer than usual. Check the terminal where you ran
step_platform.stage_stalled_prefix: Nothing coming through yet. If you're not comfortable with the terminal,
step_platform.stage_stalled_suffix: is the smoother path — one click and this computer is connected. Close this dialog and pick Desktop, or hit Skip to continue.
